name: Kinematic Swept Volume Voxelization
description: A robotics safety engineer needs to determine the swept volume of a robot arm for workcell design. Using a 6-DOF or 7-DOF robot model (such as a UR5) in CoppeliaSim and the ZMQ Remote API, calculate the unique voxels occupied during a trajectory spanning at least 60 degrees across two joints. The analysis must include 50 waypoints with 5 interpolated points between joint handles to represent physical links. Use a voxel resolution of 0.05 meters (index = round(coordinate / 0.05)) and ensure at least 100 unique voxels are recorded. Export the data to ~/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/swept_voxels.csv with columns: voxel_i, voxel_j, voxel_k, center_x, center_y, center_z (where center coordinates equal index * 0.05). Additionally, generate ~/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/volume_report.json with fields: voxel_resolution_m, total_occupied_voxels, estimated_volume_m3 (total_occupied_voxels * 0.05^3), bounding_box_min_m, bounding_box_max_m, and max_joint_ranges_swept_deg (a dictionary of joint names to their total swept ranges in degrees).
